risk_rationale: Defines the checkpoint-pointer format (segment number + in-segment byte offset) and manifest.json's on-disk JSON schema that subtask 1.3.4's recovery replay will directly depend on to know where to resume reading the WAL. Getting the offset semantics wrong (e.g. global vs per-segment) would silently corrupt recovery correctness later, even though this subtask's own tests would still pass.

downstream_dependents.reason: Recovery replay must interpret the checkpoint pointer (segment number, in-segment offset) produced here to know where to resume reading the WAL forward from.

used_symbols: none directly required by checkpoint.go; TypedRecord/AppendAndApply used only in checkpoint_test.go to write records

backward_compatibility: Additive only; no existing exported symbol in engine/wal is modified or removed.
regression_surface: None to existing writer.go/record.go behavior. New surface: manifest.json file introduced in the WAL directory; must not collide with wal-<N>.log segment file naming (confirmed disjoint: manifest.json does not match segmentFilePrefix/segmentFileSuffix).
open_items_not_addressed: docs/LLD/wal.md remains scaffold-only (flagged again, not blocking per task instructions).
RecordTypeInvalid=0 validation gap (038-verification) is 1.3.4's concern, not touched here.
Actual deletion/archival of segments identified by ArchivableSegments is out of scope; only identification is implemented.
